command: SELECT {COL1}, {COL2} FROM {TABLE} WHERE {CONDITION} LIMIT {N};
title: SELECT with WHERE and LIMIT
description: Basic query. Always use LIMIT during development — production tables can have millions of rows.

command: INSERT INTO {TABLE} ({COL1}, {COL2}) VALUES ({VAL1}, {VAL2});
title: INSERT a row
description: Insert a single row. For bulk inserts, use multiple VALUES tuples or COPY (PostgreSQL).

command: UPDATE {TABLE} SET {COL} = {VALUE} WHERE {CONDITION};
title: UPDATE rows
description: Update rows matching condition. Always include WHERE. Forgetting WHERE updates every row in the table.

command: DELETE FROM {TABLE} WHERE {CONDITION};
title: DELETE rows
description: Delete rows matching condition. Always include WHERE. Use BEGIN/ROLLBACK to test first.

command:
INSERT INTO {TABLE} ({COL1}, {COL2})
VALUES ({VAL1}, {VAL2})
ON CONFLICT ({COL1})
DO UPDATE SET {COL2} = EXCLUDED.{COL2};
title: Upsert (INSERT or UPDATE)
description: Insert if not exists, update if conflict. PostgreSQL syntax. EXCLUDED refers to the row that failed to insert.

title: INNER JOIN
description: Returns only rows with matching records in both tables. No match = row excluded from result.

title: LEFT JOIN
description: Returns all rows from left table, matched rows from right. Right side is NULL when no match.

title: RIGHT JOIN
description: Returns all rows from right table, matched rows from left. Left side is NULL when no match.

title: Self JOIN
description: Join a table with itself. Classic use: employee-manager hierarchy where both columns reference the same table.

title: GROUP BY with COUNT
description: Count occurrences of each value. The foundation of analytics queries.

title: HAVING (filter aggregates)
description: Filter groups after aggregation. WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after.

command:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  AVG({COL}) AS avg_val,
  MAX({COL}) AS max_val,
  MIN({COL}) AS min_val
FROM {TABLE};
title: Aggregate functions
description: Common aggregate functions in one query. SUM() is also available. NULL values are ignored in AVG/MAX/MIN.

title: CTE (Common Table Expression)
description: Named temporary result set. Makes complex queries readable. Can be referenced multiple times in the main query.

title: Subquery with IN
description: Filter rows using a subquery result. Use EXISTS for better performance on large datasets.

title: ROW_NUMBER window function
description: Assign sequential numbers within each partition. Filter WHERE rn = 1 to get the top row per group.

command:
SELECT
  *,
  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY {COL} DESC) AS rank
FROM {TABLE};
title: RANK window function
description: Assign rank with gaps for ties (1,1,3). Use DENSE_RANK() for no gaps (1,1,2).

command:
SELECT
  {COL},
  LAG({COL}) OVER (ORDER BY {ORDER_COL}) AS prev_val,
  LEAD({COL}) OVER (ORDER BY {ORDER_COL}) AS next_val
FROM {TABLE};
title: LAG / LEAD window functions
description: Access previous or next row's value. Essential for time-series analysis and calculating deltas.

title: CREATE TABLE
description: Create table with auto-increment PK and timestamp. IF NOT EXISTS prevents error if table already exists. PostgreSQL syntax (SERIAL).

command: ALTER TABLE {TABLE} ADD COLUMN {COL} {TYPE};
title: ALTER TABLE — add column
description: Add a new column to an existing table. Existing rows get NULL for the new column unless DEFAULT is specified.

command: ALTER TABLE {TABLE} DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS {COL};
title: ALTER TABLE — drop column
description: Remove a column. IF EXISTS prevents error if column doesn't exist. Irreversible without a backup.

command:
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_{TABLE}_{COL}
ON {TABLE}({COL});
title: CREATE INDEX (non-blocking)
description: Create an index without locking the table. CONCURRENTLY takes longer but doesn't block reads/writes in production.

command: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS {TABLE} CASCADE;
title: DROP TABLE
description: Delete a table and all its data. CASCADE also drops dependent objects (views, foreign keys). Irreversible.

command:
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)
SELECT * FROM {TABLE} WHERE {CONDITION};
title: EXPLAIN ANALYZE (query plan)
description: Show execution plan with actual timing and buffer usage. ANALYZE runs the query — use with caution on mutating queries.

title: Transaction (BEGIN / COMMIT)
description: Wrap operations in a transaction. All-or-nothing. If anything fails, ROLLBACK undoes everything. Use to safely test destructive queries.

command: SELECT COALESCE({COL}, {DEFAULT_VALUE}) AS {ALIAS} FROM {TABLE};
title: COALESCE (null fallback)
description: Return first non-NULL value. Useful for providing defaults when a column might be NULL.
